column19: Scheme Twists in the Villain Deck
column23: Comments

column2: Macho Gomez's Gladiator Pits of Sakaar
column23: lost the first try, but had more luck the 2nd go
The HQ got locked up in the first few turns with 3 rares, but eventually I had Thing and Human Torch’s rares and hit Macho 3 times in one turn!
Cap was key, I was so glad I chose him. The Avengers definitely ran smoother than the Fantastic Four here. My wife was even able to pull a 6 color combo with the Perfect Teamwork.
Forge with almost all tech deck can destroy anyone

After trying to decide on the 2 heroes,  I ultimately decided to go SHIELD.   Nick Fury's rare did not help at all as most SHIELD cards went undercover not KOed. All the tech won the game. Iron Man and the Arc Reactor was giving me 10+ attack each turn. Never could hit Blob as no xmen. He just stayed in the streets most of the game.

The initial HQ included two Repulsor Rays and I was hesitant to take them since my plan and hero choice was to go tech and tech/strength for Hulkbuster transformations.  However, after grabbing a couple Seek The Nega-Bands and transforming them into Captain Marvel, the ranged synergy made the right side very strong.  Left side... two Legendary Commanders with Deathlok SHIELD level thinning.
Really really hard setup! My first play through I lost on turn 29 but my second go-around I hit it out of the park. Side note* I defeated Galactus with a Final Blow using Galactus Hungers 😂😂😂
Epic setup! The usually "easy" Scheme got very difficult with all that tough Villains; Super-Skrull & Cap's time travels saved the day!
On attempt #2 to better my score, Morg ended up with all the bystanders and then Galactus ate his city space causing instant loss. Good thing the first attempt went better.
Unfortunately I was not able to actively play this round because I am on tour and didn't bring these cards with me. Looking forward to competing in the lower bracket!!

My first try went so bad i didnt even finish it, literally everything went wrong, cards drawn in worst possible order, constantly short 1 attack or recruit, it was a nightmare (rated 1 for Fun). In the second one Iceman finally did what he does and slapped Mephisto for 45 ^^ (rated 5 for Fun, therefore an average of 3 goes on record)
The turn I would have won the game was the turn the losing Twist came out. Such is life.
Could Not Play :-(

column2: The Sentry Floods the Planet with Melted Glaciers
column23: Really fun setup, no rares were recruited through the entire game, Left hand was focused on lightshow and some USM, right hand went with full ranged deck focusing on Havok. On turn 25 I've managed to generate 12 attack and 9 PE which was enough to defeat 3 villains and do the FInal Blow on the MM.

In hindsight, I seriously underestimated the number of wounds this mastermind generates. I probably should've just gone with a wolverine but I was hoping for synergy with havoc. Unfortunately, he barely bothered to show up. I had a good game though.
We often overlook Legion, cause he is a bit all over the place. But when he comes to play. It just works.

Pretty fun setup. Jubilee works surprisingly well with Ultimate Spider-Man. And having one hand focus on lower cost cards really helped mitigate the Flood the Planet Scheme. Sentry is always a fun fight with a setup that really changes the early game. The only pain point was Havok. I typically like playing Havok and felt like there was a lot of ranged with Invisible Woman and Jubilee, put he just didn't click. I even discarded some key cards to his beserk at unfortunate times. The other heroes I had considered were Captain Marvel and Magik. Both have a lot of ranged and Magik works well with low cost cards / sidekicks. Ultimately I'm glad I picked the safer choice of F4. Invisible Woman's KO card enabled the early game. Mr. Fantastic's uncommon is very strong and helped me wrap up the game up with a 27 fight turn.
First time playing with The Sentry and starting with wounds is tough.

column2: The Goblin Splices Humans with Spider DNA
column23: This was a puzzle, first time I couldn't get better than a draw. Second time it went a lot better. If the bystander stack is empty, you can't get a hidden witness through ambush!

Just watched No Way Home and thought triple spider-men would be thematically great against Goblin and the spider-foes of different dimensions but it was honorary spider-friend Black Widow who actually did all the work as she ate up the bystander / hidden witness deck.
Had a lot of fun with this setup. Was good to use both the sets i think i have probably played the least.

column2: Trapped in the Insane Asylum by Authoritarian Iron Man
column23: Was an interesting scheme I never played before was interesting playing cards not by my own choosing, luckily enough I only had one scheme show up then I sent it to the side where I didn’t really care what was played first.
Picked Storm to try to mitigate Iron Man's fortification effects, but the scheme kept making me play things in the wrong order. Was short 1 attack like 3 times...

Setup was (I think) impossible to win. (Tried later with different Hero combos with even less success (at least I snagged 2 tactics)). It's not a lot of fun playing 4 cards mechanically, especially when you see a really combo destroyed by card order. This was my 2nd playthrough (much better than first) and was lucky with Scheme Twist 5 being the last card in the Villain Deck.

column2: Magus's Pulse Waves from the Negative Zone

column23: Nebula really help me with KOing, second player has Gamora generating shards with Ronan. The game was in really good shape to finish it with win, unfortunately 9th Scheme Twists killed me. But KOing and using shards was fun!

column2: M.O.D.O.K.'s Portals to the Dark Dimension
column23: Man Emma Frost's rare was perfect for this setup. Using attack to fight villains to gain recruit to hit Modok's transformed side was perfect. Two times this game I got to hit him with recruit and then again with attack in the same turn. Jean Grey, Emma Frost, and Gambit are just so good together. Much better than my first attempt lol.
Didn’t hit MM until turn 31, proceeded to hit him all 5 times in the last 2 turns! Was able to build enough Atk and Rec on both sides to ping pong him between his 2 sides! Really fun hero deck!

Had lots of fun with this one. Completely forgot OUTWIT used cost and chose my heroes thinking it depended on CLASS. D’oh! In the end, it came down to the final blow. I had enough to take out MODOK and win the game, but opted to clear some villains from the city and take one or two more turn. Still had 13 villain cards in the deck. Big mistake. The following card has the 7th and final game ending scheme twist. What a rush. I love this game. Thanks for setup, Bagel!
Carnage feasted on both "Leap From Above" and "Sting Of The Spider" in the same game (one turn after recruiting them)! Glad I stuck to the theme for all the Spiders!
This gave me whole new appreciation on feast. And on Gambit in managing feast.

This is my second game score. because I went in with the strategy of having a hand dedicated to buying 2 cost cards and maximize Symbiote Spiderman, with the other hand being a flex hand, I chose banner and Hulkbuster because they both have 2 cost cards as well as to maximize Outwit on 4 of the 5 heroes I used. Overall, fun game and fairly quick. I hope I dont get stomped by the other players too much.
We enjoyed this setup and are looking forward to the next one!
Bad first game, rough second, but pulled through for the win.
Probably not moving on due to some horrible luck, but I felt good about my plan!

column2: The Hood's Super Hero Civil War
column23: WaspWoman going all Tech with Forge and Iron Man gave Hood +1 max for Dark Memories, and AntDude’s artifact heavy deck proved immune to his master strike. BFG is not only for Ronald Dahl enthusiasts, but ended the game with 16 VP earned!
Not a fan of this scheme, phew
Took 2 games, I got demolished in game 1 but finally got a good rhythm in game 2. Really interesting and fun setup
